title: stylisma aquatica
intro: Stylisma aquatica, also known as Sea Lavender, is a perennial flowering plant native to the southeastern United States and is part of the Convolvulaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of essential oils, including geraniol and borneol. The medicinal properties of Stylisma aquatica have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, as well as providing relief from stress and anxiety. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in tea, tinctures, and salves, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
